message: 🌱 Add linkerd: multicluster doesn't work on EKS mission (#375)

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,85 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "linkerd-4582-multicluster-doesn-t-work-on-eks",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "linkerd: multicluster doesn't work on EKS",
+    "description": "multicluster doesn't work on EKS. Users encounter: \"```msg=\"Error processing RemoteGatewayCreated: {gatewaySpec: GatewaySpec: {gatewayName: linkerd-gate\".",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify linkerd feature symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your linkerd deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=linkerd\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for error: ````msg=\"Error processing RemoteGatewayCreated: {gatewaySpec: GatewaySpec: {gatewayName: linkerd-gateway, gatewayNamespac`"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Check current Service configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant linkerd resources:\n```bash\nkubectl get service -A\nkubectl describe service <name> -n <namespace>\n```\nWhen attempting to link a source cluster to a target EKS cluster using the `linkerd multicluster link` command, the service mirror controller in the source cluster logs this error:\n\n```msg=\"Error proc"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for multicluster doesn't work on EKS",
+        "description": "### Issue: #4501 \n\nAfter a few attempts at adding support for `EndpointSlice` resources, I came up with this poc to show one way of doing this.\n\n### EndpointSlice access\n---\n\n* The first prerequisite to supporting `EndpointSlices` is to make sure that the cluster has access to the resource, since it\n```yaml\nand fails to create the endpoints object for the gateway mirror.\r\n\r\nThis happens because the service mirror controller attempts to copy the `hostname` and `IP` fields from the gateway service's ingress struct to the EndpointAddress struct of the gateway mirror's endpoints object.  In EKS, the ingress struct of the `linkerd-gateway` service is populated with a `hostname` and no `IP`.  However, the `IP` field of EndpointAddress is mandatory.\n### Issue: #4501 \r\n\r\nAfter a few attempts at adding support for `EndpointSlice` resources, I came up with this poc to show one way of doing this.\r\n\r\n\r\n### E\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade linkerd to include the fix",
+        "description": "If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade linkerd:\n```bash\nhelm repo update\nhelm upgrade linkerd jetstack/linkerd --namespace cert-manager\n```\nVerify the upgrade:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n cert-manager\nhelm list -n cert-manager\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm multicluster doesn't work on EKS is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m\nkubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm that ````msg=\"Error processing RemoteGatewayCreated: {gatewaySpec: GatewaySpec: {gatewayName: linkerd-gateway, gatewayNamespac` no longer appears in logs."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "The root cause is: ### Issue: #4501 \n\nAfter a few attempts at adding support for `EndpointSlice` resources, I came up with this poc to show one way of doing this.\n\n### EndpointSlice access\n---\n\n* The first prerequisite to supporting `EndpointSlices` is to make sure that the cluster has access to the resource, since it sits behind a feature gate. To do this, we are using a similar check to `ServiceProfile`. First, we. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "and fails to create the endpoints object for the gateway mirror.\r\n\r\nThis happens because the service mirror controller attempts to copy the `hostname` and `IP` fields from the gateway service's ingress struct to the EndpointAddress struct of the gateway mirror's endpoints object.  In EKS, the ingress struct of the `linkerd-gateway` service is populated with a `hostname` and no `IP`.  However, the `IP` field of EndpointAddress is mandatory.\n### Issue: #4501 \r\n\r\nAfter a few attempts at adding support for `EndpointSlice` resources, I came up with this poc to show one way of doing this.\r\n\r\n\r\n### EndpointSlice access\r\n---\r\n\r\n* The first prerequisite to supporting `EndpointSlices` is to make sure that the cluster has access to the resource, since it sits behind a feature gate. To do this, we are",
+        "* `EndpointSlices` ARE a subset. Instead of grouping all subsets into a single resource, the slice contains only a set of addresses with common parts.",
+        "and like this in the case where we do not have an IP (EKS)"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
